@@ -609,6 +609,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-e8kp1","title":"add mounted-path ext4 readlink-on-non-symlink EINVAL coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path tests prove readlink success on real symlinks, but they do not freeze the public error contract for calling readlink on an ordinary file or directory. ffs-core rejects non-symlink inodes and the mounted surface should present Linux EINVAL. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that call readlink on non-symlink paths and assert exact EINVAL without mutating metadata. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 non-symlink readlink refusal behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:02.498095050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:43:15.075752349Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:43:15.073875805Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ece","title":"ffs-block: Cache metrics + instrumentation surface","description":"Goal: expose cache behavior to harness/CLI/TUI so performance work is evidence-based.

Deliverables:
- CacheMetrics struct: hit/miss counts, evictions, current list sizes, p value.
- A cheap snapshot method to read metrics without heavy locks.
- Optional tracing hooks gated behind feature flag.

Acceptance:
- Metrics can be queried from ffs-core and printed by ffs-cli inspect.
- Metrics are used by benchmarks to assert improvements are real (not noise).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:53.854812013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:49:40.556955612Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:49:40.556929884Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ece","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:26.979686708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-edqo","title":"Add load_evidence_records preset+tail coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:42:44.576977883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:20.261225902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:20.260960850Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-efc2j","title":"[HIGH] ext4 e2compr conformance regressed: inline-data flag remains set on gzip write path","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `ext4_e2compr_write_readback_conforms_for_gzip_and_lzo` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:1793`
- assertion: `gzip: e2compr setup must clear inline-data before write-side conformance`
- left: `268435456`
- right: `0`

This looks like a recurrence of the earlier inline-data clearing fix, but the current workspace gate is still red on the gzip path.

Acceptance:
- gzip and lzo write-side conformance both pass
- inline-data flag is cleared before e2compr write-path setup
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this failure slice.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:24:44.522462071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:24:44.522462071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2compr","ext4","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-efuu","title":"Wire btrfs write path through FsOps","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:05:49.507373563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T18:57:52.401319799Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T18:57:52.401237896Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-eiae2","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_repair_evidence_ledger target","description":"ffs_repair::parse_evidence_ledger (evidence.rs:845) - create new fuzz target","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:48:16.517799303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:48:16.517799303Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-eicp","title":"Fix ext4 e2compr rewrite free-space accounting","description":"Full ffs-harness conformance run on 2026-04-18 fails ext4_e2compr_write_readback_conforms_for_gzip_and_lzo: gzip rewrite should reuse compressed blocks without leaking space, but free-space accounting differs by one block. Audit e2compr rewrite allocation/free logic and conformance fixture accounting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:00:56.472608017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:33.401953712Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:33.399841023Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed stale MVCC bitmap snapshots in compressed ext4 allocation/free paths and preserved orphan traversal via raw inode reads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2compr","ext4"]}
@@ -617,6 +618,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-eptw","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore local benchmark scratch artifacts","description":"Context: bv triage reports all existing beads closed (no actionable work). Current workspace repeatedly accumulates untracked local-only benchmark artifacts (loopback ext4 images, mount staging dirs, local sqlite backups) that create noise and accidental-commit risk for multi-agent work.\n\nScope:\n- Add targeted .gitignore rules for local benchmark scratch artifacts only.\n- Do not ignore tracked benchmark baselines or fixture assets.\n- Validate rules via git check-ignore on representative paths.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Existing untracked scratch paths are ignored by git.\n- Tracked baseline files remain tracked/unaffected.\n- Agent-mail update posted with bead id and completion summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:53:17.689900464Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309714130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309693260Z","close_reason":"Completed: scratch artifact ignore rules added and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-coordination","benchmark","ci","hygiene"],"comments":[{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-eptw","author":"SilverLake","text":"Implemented targeted .gitignore rules for benchmark scratch artifacts and local sqlite backups; validated with git check-ignore for mount_probe.ext4, mount_recovery_probe.ext4, mount_probe_mounts/, ffs_cli_inspect_probe.stderr, and storage.sqlite3.bak. Confirmed tracked baseline file baselines/hyperfine/20260218/ffs_cli_parity.json is not ignored.","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-etxp9","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for unlink rmdir and rename","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e covers unlink, rmdir, and rename success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only refusal contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse rejects these namespace mutations with EROFS before dispatch. Scope: add read-only btrfs probes for unlink, rmdir, and rename that assert exact EROFS and unchanged directory entries. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for unlink, rmdir, and rename.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:27:15.710372657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-evou","title":"Trim unused dependencies in ffs-dir/ffs-xattr/ffs-ondisk manifests","description":"Explorer audit found likely unused manifest deps: ffs-dir declares ffs-types/ffs-block/ffs-inode/asupersync but source uses only ffs_error + ffs_ondisk; ffs-xattr declares ffs-block but source does not use it; ffs-ondisk declares ffs-error but parser crate appears to use ParseError from ffs-types only. Remove unused deps, keep crate boundaries clean, and validate with rch-offloaded cargo check/clippy/fmt gates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:46:10.093749421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:34:22.114790888Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:34:22.114769979Z","close_reason":"Verified: manifests already trimmed (ffs-dir: only ffs-error+ffs-ondisk; ffs-xattr: ffs-types+ffs-error+ffs-ondisk; ffs-ondisk: ffs-types+crc32c+serde). cargo check passes cleanly via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ew490","title":"[HIGH] btrfs tamper-detection conformance still fails tree_block_csum validation at open","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failures:
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:1103`
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` fails on the same root cause
- error: `open btrfs image: Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`

This is narrower than the umbrella conformance bead: the synthetic image used by the tamper-detection contract is still rejected before the actual tamper-detection assertion can run.

Acceptance:
- tamper-detection fixture opens successfully before the intentional corruption step
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this btrfs checksum slice.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:26:03.678353852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:26:03.678353852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance","crc","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-ewdrq","title":"add mounted-path ext4 getxattr/listxattr size-probe and ERANGE coverage","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse already implements Linux xattr size-probe and ERANGE behavior via classify_xattr_reply/reply_xattr_payload, but fuse_e2e only exercises successful payload reads. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes for getxattr and listxattr with size=0 length discovery plus undersized-buffer ERANGE on real FUSE paths; assert exact reported lengths, exact errno, and no state mutation. Acceptance: ext4 E2E proves size-probe and too-small-buffer behavior for both getxattr and listxattr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:07.763102031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-f4lmp","title":"Add golden contract for representative btrfs sys_chunk parsing and logical mapping","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:32:44.030086267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:41:52.889447072Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T23:41:52.887062540Z","close_reason":"Added representative btrfs sys_chunk parsing and logical mapping golden contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing-golden-artifacts"]}
 {"id":"bd-f5kt","title":"Add doc comments to ffs-core public API (FsOps, OpenFs, Ext4FsOps)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T18:48:28.616099772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T19:53:23.584154673Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T19:53:23.584043054Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -1075,6 +1077,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-r7di0","title":"Add metamorphic coverage for inode checksum field-content invariance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:03:31.039875346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:39.221685045Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:39.219639844Z","close_reason":"Added ffs-core metamorphic target for inode checksum field-content invariance; rch fmt/check/clippy/test passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r7od","title":"Add generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Upstream xfstests generic/112 runs fsx with -A and -x to exercise AIO plus preallocated unwritten extents. FrankenFS already has fallocate tests, but the harness lacks an explicit cross-filesystem conformance check for the preallocation contract that generic/112 depends on. Add deterministic ext4+btrfs harness coverage proving that preallocation extends size, unwritten regions read as zeroes, writes into preallocated space preserve surrounding zero-fill, and the contract survives reopen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:23:32.225284126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.368172019Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.365241100Z","close_reason":"Added generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs via shared harness assertions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r7vx0","title":"[HIGH] restore missing fuzz_ioctl_dispatch corpus dir for crash promotion pipeline","description":"Workspace gate regression from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22 via `rch` worker `vmi1156319`:

- `crash_promotion::tests::corpus_directories_exist` panics because `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch` is missing
- `crash_promotion::tests::pipeline_validation_passes` then fails with `22/23 corpus directories found`

Root cause is repo drift between the fuzz target inventory and the shipped corpus directories. Acceptance:
1. `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch/` exists with at least one seed or the pipeline no longer requires it.
2. `crates/ffs-harness/src/crash_promotion.rs` inventory and repo layout are consistent.
3. `cargo test -p ffs-harness crash_promotion -- --nocapture` passes via `rch`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-r8c8r","title":"[HIGH] fixture checksum manifest drifted for ext4 inline-data continuation JSON","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:3680`
- fixture: `ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json`
- manifest digest: `b040217daafc0d5a7706a9263652b33776989f5b7c5c7f76e807a243860f6218`
- actual digest: `5f811167369ea9e1bee12196a8ce72f070cd08105d85473401e22dc28a646525`

The fixture content and checksum manifest are out of sync, so the conformance gate stays red even when parser behavior is otherwise stable.

Acceptance:
- fixture and checksum manifest agree again
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-r9n2u","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for path-component validation","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs::validate_single_path_component(name: &[u8]). Scope: add a very high-throughput libFuzzer target over raw path bytes, seeded with valid ASCII names plus boundary cases for empty, dot, dot-dot, slash-containing, NUL-containing, overlong, and non-UTF8 names. Assert totality (no panics), deterministic errno-class mapping, and compatibility with the callers that use this gate for create, link, symlink, rename, and xattr-adjacent path mutations. Acceptance: new fuzz target registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, corpus seeds added, and any minimized crashers promoted to regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:59:00Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","fuzz","paths","testing","validation"]}
 {"id":"bd-raks","title":"Fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation by updating stale `FsGeometry` initializers in `ffs-inode` to include the required `desc_size` and `feature_ro_compat` fields.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the current `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` failure via `rch`.
2. Patch the failing `FsGeometry` initializers in `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs`.
3. Re-run targeted `rch` checks for `ffs-inode` and, if clean, the workspace check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs` compiles with current `FsGeometry` definition
- Targeted `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets` passes
- Workspace check moves past this failure or reports the next unrelated blocker
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:40:47.448066803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.994009750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.993264249Z","close_reason":"Patched missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests; targeted rch cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","inode","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-rale","title":"Improve FfsError::Io errno mapping with ErrorKind fallback","description":"When std::io::Error has no raw_os_error() (e.g. created via Error::new()), to_errno() falls back to EIO. Improve by mapping io::ErrorKind to appropriate errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, etc.) before falling back to EIO. This gives FUSE callers more specific error codes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T00:49:02.204994325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073573327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073546988Z","close_reason":"Improved to_errno() Io variant: now maps ErrorKind to specific errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, AlreadyExists→EEXIST, WouldBlock→EAGAIN, InvalidInput/InvalidData→EINVAL, TimedOut→ETIMEDOUT, Interrupted→EINTR) when raw_os_error() is None, instead of always falling back to EIO. Added test io_error_kind_mapping_without_raw_errno covering all mapped kinds. Also fixed two pre-existing clippy pedantic lints. All 20 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
